summary_en: FY3/26 results: ROE 6.7%, PBR 0.62x. Company attributes low valuation to weak domestic demand in core divisions (drawn copper, precision parts, piping/plating), insufficient retained earnings, and unmet shareholder return expectations. Medium-term target: PBR ≥1.0x via (1) revenue growth from Mitani Shinten integration, high-value product expansion, group procurement cost reduction; (2) M&A-led growth investment, progressive dividend policy, capital-efficiency focus; (3) enhanced IR (English disclosure, investor dialogue).
title_en: Update on Measures to Realize Management Focused on Capital Cost and Share Price
translation_en_brief: On 21 May 2026, CK San-Etsu announced an update to its May 2025 capital-cost-conscious management plan. FY3/26 results show ROE of 6.7% and PBR of 0.62x, both below targets. The company attributes the low PBR to weak domestic demand in its core divisions (drawn copper, precision components, piping and plating), historically insufficient retained earnings, and failure to meet shareholder return expectations. The company identifies two key levers: revenue growth (integration synergies from Mitani Shinten acquisition completed April 2025, expanding high-margin products, group procurement savings) and shareholder returns (M&A investment, progressive dividend policy, capital-efficiency discipline). Medium-term target is PBR ≥1.0x, supported by strengthened English-language disclosure and enhanced dialogue with institutional investors.
